title: Diagnose a shader compile or link failure
description: Read the compiler/validation diagnostics and reconcile interface mismatches between pipeline stages.
body: Steps: 1. Read the full compiler log; the first error usually causes the cascade. 2. Check stage interface matching: vertex outputs must match fragment inputs by location/name and type. 3. Verify uniform/descriptor bindings match the pipeline layout. 4. Confirm the shading language version and required extensions are declared. 5. Recompile through the validation/SPIR-V toolchain to catch undefined behavior the driver tolerates. Rollback point: before editing the pipeline layout. Validation: shaders compile and link clean and the validation layer reports no interface mismatch; do not silence a warning by deleting the offending output without checking the consumer stage.
